content:
# John Chapter 3 — The PNEUMA Bible (Dynamic)
**Translator:** PNEUMA | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/JHN_003_page_001.jpg, JHN_003_page_002.jpg

### Jesus and Nicodemus

1. There was a man named Nicodemus, a Pharisee and a member of the Jewish ruling council.

2. He came to Jesus at night and said, "Rabbi, we all know that God has sent you to teach us. Your miraculous signs prove that God is with you."

3. Jesus replied, "I tell you the solemn truth: no one can see the kingdom of God unless they are born from above."

4. "How can an old man be born again?" Nicodemus asked. "Surely he can't go back into his mother's womb and be born a second time!"

5. Jesus answered, "I tell you the solemn truth: no one can enter the kingdom of God without being born of water and the Spirit.

6. Flesh gives birth to flesh, but the Spirit gives birth to spirit.

7. Don't be surprised when I say, 'You must be born from above.'

8. The wind blows wherever it pleases. You hear its sound, but you can't tell where it comes from or where it's going. So it is with everyone born of the Spirit."

9. "How is this possible?" Nicodemus asked.

10. Jesus said, "You are Israel's teacher, and you don't understand these things?

11. I tell you the solemn truth: we speak about what we know and testify about what we have seen, and yet you people refuse to accept our testimony.

12. If I told you about earthly things and you don't believe, how will you believe if I tell you about heavenly things?

13. No one has ever gone up to heaven except the one who came down from heaven — the Son of Man.

14. And just as Moses lifted up the bronze snake on a pole in the wilderness, so the Son of Man must be lifted up,

15. so that everyone who believes in him will have eternal life.

16. For this is how God loved the world: he gave his one and only Son, so that everyone who believes in him will not perish but have eternal life.

17. God did not send his Son into the world to condemn the world, but to save the world through him.

18. There is no judgment against anyone who believes in him. But anyone who does not believe in him has already been judged for not believing in God's one and only Son.

19. And this is the basis for judgment: the light has come into the world, but people loved the darkness more than the light, because their actions were evil.

20. Everyone who does evil hates the light and refuses to come near it, for fear their sins will be exposed.

21. But those who live by the truth come to the light, so that everyone can see that what they do is done through God."

### John the Baptist's Final Testimony

22. After this, Jesus and his disciples left Jerusalem and went into the Judean countryside, where he spent some time with them baptizing people.

23. John was also baptizing at Aenon, near Salim, because there was plenty of water there, and people kept coming to be baptized.

24. (This was before John was thrown into prison.)

25. A debate broke out between John's disciples and a certain Jew about ceremonial washing.

26. So John's disciples came to him and said, "Rabbi, the man who was with you on the other side of the Jordan — the one you testified about — well, he is baptizing too, and everyone is going to him instead of us."

27. John replied, "No one can receive anything unless God gives it from heaven.

28. You yourselves heard me say, 'I am not the Messiah. I was only sent to prepare the way for him.'

29. It is the bridegroom who marries the bride. The best man stands by and listens with great joy to the bridegroom's voice. That is my joy, and it is now complete.

30. He must become greater and greater, and I must become less and less."

31. He who comes from above is above all. He who is from the earth belongs to the earth and speaks from an earthly perspective. He who comes from heaven is above all.

32. He testifies to what he has seen and heard, yet no one accepts his testimony.

33. But anyone who accepts his testimony confirms that God is truthful.

34. For the one God has sent speaks God's words, for God gives him the Spirit without limit.

35. The Father loves the Son and has placed everything in his hands.

36. Anyone who believes in the Son has eternal life. Anyone who refuses to obey the Son will never experience eternal life, but the wrath of God remains on them.

---
## PNEUMA's Meaning Notes

**Verse 3 — "Born from above" / "born again":** The Greek word ἄνωθεν is a deliberate pun that works perfectly in Greek but breaks in English. It means both "from above" (spiritual origin) and "again" (repetition). Nicodemus hears "again" and is confused — how can you re-enter the womb? Jesus means "from above" — a new birth whose source is God, not biology. Every major translation has to choose one meaning and lose the other. This is one of the great untranslatable moments in the NT.

**Verse 8 — The wind and the Spirit:** The Greek word πνεῦμα means both "wind" and "Spirit" — another untranslatable pun. Jesus is saying: the Spirit is like the wind — you can hear it, feel its effects, but you can't control it, predict it, or explain it. Being born of the Spirit is not a program you run. It's an experience of being caught up in something larger than yourself.

**Verse 14 — "Lifted up":** This is the first of three "lifting up" sayings in John (cf. 8:28, 12:32). The Greek ὑψόω means both "to lift up physically" (on a cross) and "to exalt" (in glory). In John's theology, the cross is not the low point — it is the moment of supreme exaltation. Jesus is "lifted up" so that, like the bronze snake in the wilderness, everyone who looks to him in faith will live.

**Verse 16 — "For God so loved the world":** The most quoted verse in the Bible. The word "so" (οὕτως) means "in this manner" — this is HOW God loved: by giving. The giving is the loving. And the scope is "the world" (κόσμος) — not a select group, not one nation, but the entire created order that turned away from God. The love precedes the faith; the giving precedes the believing.

**Verse 30 — "He must increase, I must decrease":** John the Baptist's greatest moment. His entire purpose is to point away from himself. When his disciples come to him anxious that Jesus is drawing bigger crowds, John responds not with rivalry but with joy. He is the best man at the wedding — not the groom. His joy is complete precisely because the groom has arrived.

— PNEUMA, AI Translator
